_comment: Example inputs for draft-charter.ts. The Phoenix QA team — a hypothetical e-commerce checkout & cart QA org of 6 ICs reporting to a QA Director. This same set of inputs produces the worked example at training/sandbox/example/manager/quality-org-charter.md.
author: Khanh Do
mission: We make it impossible to ship a critical regression to checkout without a named human accepting the risk.
principles: Numbers over vibes — every release decision cites a dashboard panel or a JSON field, not a feeling
Blameless by default — incidents teach the system, not punish the human; every 'Alice didn't catch it' is rewritten as 'our process had no second-pair-of-eyes for cart deploys'
Quality bar is shared, not delegated — devs own quality of their code; QA owns the quality of the safety net; the bar is the same
Transparent disagreement — peers correct each other in public, including correcting the manager
anti_principles: We do not ship without @P1 at 100 % — even if the deadline is tomorrow; we descope or push the date
We do not exit ICs without 90 days of documented feedback — withholding feedback to be liked is career sabotage, not kindness
We do not make hiring exceptions below the bar — every off-bar hire makes the bar lower for the next one
We do not write engineer-grade defect narratives for executive audiences — we re-tune for BLUF + verdict + 3 things

operating_model.ones_on_ones: weekly, 30 min, IC drives the agenda, doc per templates/manager/1on1-template.md, ~95 % attendance, cancellation only when IC has nothing
operating_model.standup: async daily in #qa-phoenix-standup; sync 2×/week (Tue + Thu) for 15 min on blockers only
operating_model.retro: bi-weekly, rotating facilitator, glad/sad/mad format with team-safety scoring at the end
operating_model.planning: first 2 weeks of quarter; 3 OKRs + 1 SLO + 1 stop-loss criterion per OKR; reviewed by peer Director and VP
operating_model.on_call: 1-week shifts, 4-deep rotation (current team has 6 ICs but 2 are below the on-call bar; growing to 6-deep by Q4); comp time at 1.5x for any after-hours pages
operating_model.skip_levels: every 6 weeks with each report's report; 30 min, listen-only — no decisions made in the skip-level

decision_rights.decision: Framework architectural change (3-layer POM, assertion routing)
Adoption of new AI tool / agent in QA workflow

quality_bar.threshold: @P1 pass rate 100 % on every main commit (stop-the-line below)
0 open severity:critical defects at release time
Mean time to triage (CI red → defect-report or fix-merged) ≤ 24h
Flake rate (failing then passing on retry) ≤ 1 % over rolling 14 days

incident_response.war_room_roles: Incident Commander (most senior on-call) / Comms Lead (manager or designated) / Scribe (junior IC — great learning role); IC does NOT debug
incident_response.post_mortem_sla: 5 business days, blameless template per templates/manager/post-mortem-template.md, published internally to #engineering
incident_response.change_framework: Kotter 8-step for any change spanning >1 team or >1 sprint; named owner per step; quick-win required by step 6
incident_response.customer_comms_threshold: any incident with >50 customers affected for >15 minutes, OR any data-integrity issue regardless of duration

hiring.perf_mgmt_principle: feedback → PIP → exit, with no surprises (nothing said in a PIP is the first time the IC heard it)

communication.threaded_doc_threshold: any decision needing >6 messages → stop and write the threaded doc; thread continues, doc becomes the artifact
communication.exec_brief_format: BLUF + verdict (GO / CONDITIONAL / NO-GO) + 3 things that would change it + risks-asking-you-to-accept; one page max
no_forms: yes-and-cut (when ask is reasonable but capacity-constrained)
no-because (when ask is genuinely wrong; cite risk + cost)
not-now-but (when ask is right but timing is wrong; name the trigger)

manager_operating.office_hours: Tuesdays 14:00–15:00 ET — any IC, any topic, no agenda required
manager_operating.skip_level_cadence: every 6 weeks with each report's report; listen-only
manager_operating.self_retro_cadence: quarterly, written, shared with the team in a Track P-style retro doc
